title: PostgreSQL 18 Schema, Constraints, Keys, and Query-Driven Indexes · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A delayed incident review reconstructs postgresql 18 schema, constraints, keys, and query-driven indexes from source and emitted revision, tenant, URL, validator, bytes, runtime validation, Promise settlement, transaction, lease, response, failure, handle cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for constrained TypeScript feed-service relational model after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, c…
objectives: Model tenants, principals, sources, feeds, subscriptions, entries, versions, people, categories, fetches, leases, and reader state with explicit ownership.
Use stable internal identifiers plus tenant-scoped external identities and preserve URL moves, aliases, and provenance.
Encode required fields, lengths, checks, states, unique scopes, foreign keys, cascades, and temporal rules in PostgreSQL.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.instruction:
review case — A review TypeScript team handles case 7685 during a hostile XML and unknown-value admission drill. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile relational entities, stable keys, constraints, tenant scope, and query-driven indexes; browser TypeScript must remain deterministic and free of Node, network, database, and host effects; then change one PostgreSQL constraint, transaction, migration, upsert, query, or search plan and preserve admitted state. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Feed Ts Tenant Keying, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

For separate evidence from confidence, cast one convenient XML object as a feed, omit reader and publisher outcomes, source and emitted revision, discovery and SSRF scope, Atom and namespace cases, entity and dangerous-key limits, unknown runtime validation, Fetch stream and AbortSignal ownership, PostgreSQL client, Promise settlement, tenant, accessibility, recovery, and controlled Node transfer, then accept plausible objects as feed-service proof.
